context:
Section 7 (Future Research Directions), first bullet; page 31.

"We showed that every symmetric convex body can be approximated arbitrarily well by a GE. Our approximation factor relies on results on polytopic approximation of convex bodies. A related question is: how well can one approximate an n-dimensional symmetric convex body with a finite intersection of m (co-centered) ellipsoids, with m growing potentially with n? Since we have shown that a GE-d, with d = 2m−3, can exactly represent an intersection of m co-centered ellipsoids (see Corollary 5.6 and Theorem 5.1), progress on this question can potentially improve our approximation factor in Theorem 5.7."
title: Approximating symmetric convex bodies by intersections of a limited number of co-centered ellipsoids
latex:
Let \(n\ge 2\) and let \(C\subset \mathbb{R}^n\) be a symmetric convex body, i.e., a compact convex set with nonempty interior and satisfying \(x\in C\Rightarrow -x\in C\). For a positive integer \(m\), consider families of \(m\) co-centered ellipsoids \(E_i=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid x^TQ_i x\le 1\}\), where \(Q_i\in \mathbb{S}^n_{++}\) and all ellipsoids are centered at the origin. Define their intersection \(E=\bigcap_{i=1}^m E_i\).

Determine, as a function of \(n\) and \(m\), the smallest approximation factor \(\alpha=\alpha(n,m)\ge 1\) such that for every symmetric convex body \(C\subset\mathbb{R}^n\) there exist \(m\) co-centered ellipsoids with intersection \(E\) satisfying
\[
E\subseteq C\subseteq \alpha\,E.
\]
Equivalently, characterize the best achievable worst-case multiplicative approximation of arbitrary symmetric convex bodies by finite intersections of \(m\) co-centered ellipsoids, allowing \(m\) to grow with \(n\).
location_in_paper: Section 7 (Future Research Directions), first bullet; page 31.
open_problem_quote: "We showed that every symmetric convex body can be approximated arbitrarily well by a GE. Our approximation factor relies on results on polytopic approximation of convex bodies. A related question is: how well can one approximate an n-dimensional symmetric convex body with a finite intersection of m (co-centered) ellipsoids, with m growing potentially with n? Since we have shown that a GE-d, with d = 2m−3, can exactly represent an intersection of m co-centered ellipsoids (see Corollary 5.6 and Theorem 5.1), progress on this question can potentially improve our approximation factor in Theorem 5.7."
